subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Research question is concise, specific and consistently integrated across all sections
Introduction and contextual background are comprehensive and clearly linked to the research aims
Methodology section demonstrates clear, informed selection of relevant secondary sources and financial tools
weaknesses: Minor scope drift by introducing 2021â22 data beyond the 2023 focus
Exclusive reliance on secondary data without any primary research element
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work exhibits the hallmarks of the top band: a clearly stated, wellâfocused research question; comprehensive context setting; and a clearly evidenced, appropriate methodology. Although there is slight drift into earlier years and no primary research, these are minor relative to the overall clarity and coherence. On balance, the quality justifies the full 6 marks.

strengths: Terminology (ARPU, ROI, payback period) is defined and used accurately throughout
Source materials are consistently and appropriately applied to support financial claims
Subject knowledge is clear and coherent, demonstrating effective understanding of business management and accounting concepts
weaknesses: Typographical and unitânotation errors (e.g., âmil milonesâ) detract slightly from precision
Inconsistent currency units (switching to euros without clear rationale)
Acronyms (ARPU) should be defined at first mention to improve clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All strands fall solidly in the 5â6 band: terminology use is accurate, source application is relevant, and subject knowledge is coherent. However, recurring minor errors (typos, currency inconsistencies) prevent the award of the maximum 6 marks, placing the criterion at a 5.

strengths: Research draws on multiple reputable secondary sources and triangulates data effectively
Analysis is clear and well supported by tables, calculations and logical interpretation
Arguments are logically developed, acknowledge limitations and demonstrate a critical perspective
weaknesses: Absence of any primary research limits depth of insight
No modelling of alternative scenarios or sensitivity analyses to test key assumptions
A few assertions (e.g., 55% viewing share) lack direct citation or detailed justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality is strong and consistently relevant (7â9 band), analysis is well supported (4â6 band) and the argument is well developed with partial critical evaluation (4â6 band). The combined performance sits at the high end of the 7â9 band, justifying 9 marks but not full topâband credit due to the lack of primary data and scenario modelling.

strengths: Structure follows IB requirements flawlessly, with all required sections and clear signposting
Layout, formatting and citation style are consistently applied and professionally presented
Word count, title page and page numbering are correctly implemented
weaknesses: Figures lack full axis labels and source attributions
Some tables could benefit from standardized captions for improved clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report meets all elements of the top presentation band: a clearly appropriate structure and correct layout conventions. Only very minor improvements to figure captions and labels are needed, so the full 4 marks are warranted.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is sharply delimited (impact of investment in original content on profitability in 2023) and is consistently referenced in every chapter heading.  Nevertheless, parts of the analysis introduce 2021â22 data and broader competitive issues, causing minor drift from the narrow 2023 focus.  This slight dilution prevents a perfect score but still fits the descriptor of a clearly stated, well-focused question that remains effectively connected to the discussion.

The argument is logicalâinvestment leads to higher ROI and faster paybackâand limitations (exclusive secondary data, inability to isolate licensed content) are acknowledged.  Some evaluative comments about competition and subscriber saturation add critical perspective, though they are brief and not integrated into a full alternative-strategy appraisal.  This reflects a well-developed argument with partially critical evaluation, matching the upper range of 4-6.

Discussion of ROI, amortisation policy and competitive dynamics shows solid command of Business Management theory and financial accounting principles.  Concepts are connected coherently across sections, evidencing effective understanding of the sources and the topic.  Occasional simplifications (e.g., equating âingresosâ directly with âprofitâ) limit absolute excellence but knowledge remains clear, accurate and well-structured.

Specialised termsâARPU, amortizaciÃ³n acelerada, ROI, payback periodâare defined and employed consistently throughout.  Calculations use correct formulae and units, and Spanish/English terminology is blended without loss of precision.  A few typographical slips (âGanaciaâ) appear but do not impede meaning, so usage meets the descriptor for accurate and consistent terminology.

Calculations of ROI, payback and financial ratios are correct, interpreted and then connected to strategy.  Tables and figures support each analytical step, and conclusions (e.g., positive correlation between investment and margins) are explicitly justified.  Analysis stops short of modelling alternative scenarios or adjusting for confounding variables, so it is clear and well-supported rather than truly sophisticatedâhence the top of the 4-6 band.

Tables, charts and quotations are integrated to substantiate every financial claim, indicating that source material is applied in a directly relevant manner.  References are properly attributed and numerical data are used for calculations, not merely described.  Minor instances of unsupported assertions (e.g., forecast 55 % viewing share) prevent the very highest standard but overall application is clearly relevant and appropriate.

An extensive range of credible secondary sources is cited (Form 10-K, Statista, Bloomberg, Business Insider, analyst interviews).  Financial tools (ROI, payback, margin ratios) are appropriately selected and their limitations briefly acknowledged, demonstrating informed selection.  The sole reliance on secondary data is recognised as a weakness, but the breadth, relevance and critical commentary fulfil the top-band expectation of clear evidence of effective and informed methodological choices.

The investigation draws on audited financial statements, industry reports and reputable news outlets, all directly relevant to the research question.  Data for three years are triangulated and cross-checked, demonstrating breadth and depth beyond minimum requirements.  While primary research is absent, the secondary research remains purposeful and systematically linked to profitability, warranting a strong score within the 7-9 band.

Word count, title, page numbers and consistent Harvard-style citations are provided.  Tables and graphs are labelled and formatted neatly.  Minor inconsistencies in font size within equations are negligible.  Overall layout conventions are adhered to correctly, placing the work at the top band for presentation.

The student states a concise, specific research question and repeats it verbatim on the title page, table of contents and in the introduction.  The contextual background (rise of streaming, Netflixâs strategic pivot) is fully explained and linked to profitability, showing comprehensive communication of why the topic is important.  Definitions of key terms such as âcontenido originalâ and the temporal scope (2023) are explicit, giving the reader a complete picture.  Overall, topic and rationale are handled with the clarity and completeness characteristic of the top band.

The essay follows IB EE structureâtitle page, abstract substitutes, table of contents, numbered chapters, conclusion, references and appendices.  Headings and sub-headings signpost the flow clearly, and required components are present, satisfying the descriptor for a clearly appropriate structure.

annotations.comment: The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly formatted, facilitating easy navigation through chapters and reinforcing a coherent report structure.
Entries use repetitive section titles; consider adding indenting for subheadings to improve readability.
Consider incorporating primary data or expert interviews to triangulate findings and mitigate the limitations of secondary sources.
The research question is clearly stated, well-focused on 2023 profitability and effectively anchored in the introduction, supporting precise scope and alignment with the investigation aims.
The inclusion of a precise word count at the start complies with IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to report conventions.
The methodology section clearly outlines the exclusive use of secondary sources and justifies tool selection, demonstrating informed and appropriate method choice.
The introduction effectively sets context by linking streaming trends, competitive dynamics, and strategic content investment, establishing clear relevance to the research question.
Avoid overly general statements about platform growth; tighten focus by directly linking each claim to profitability metrics.
Footnote numbering and formatting appear inconsistent with Harvard style; ensure superscript placement and citation details align with IB guidelines.
The contextual background and direct citations from Ted Sarandos anchor the financial analysis in primary source evidence, enhancing subject understanding.
Table 1 integrates financial data clearly to compare 2022 and 2023 production costs, demonstrating appropriate application of numerical source material.
Add a concise caption above the table indicating title and source to improve presentation clarity.
The figure lacks axis labels and data source notation; include them to ensure self-contained interpretation and alignment with presentation criteria.
The switch to euros for net income disrupts currency consistency; convert all figures to a single currency for coherent comparative analysis.
The term 'Ingreso netos' is pluralized incorrectly; use 'Ingresos netos' or 'Ingreso neto' for precise financial terminology.
Comparative discussion of Q3 2024 results introduces future data beyond 2023 scope; maintain strict focus on the year defined in the research question.
The cost discussion quantifies production changes, but the unspecified currency unit for '10 millones' reduces precision; specify millions in USD or local currency.
Footnote text includes HTML artifacts (<sup>&</sup>); clean metadata tags to maintain professional citation formatting.
Integration of global subscriber statistics from Statista directly supports analysis of market position and profitability context.
The use of ARPU is clear, but the acronym should be defined at first mention to ensure consistent use of financial terminology.
The chart visually communicates series viewership, yet lacks scale or legend; adding axis markers will strengthen clarity and adherence to presentation standards.
The 55% viewing share assertion is compelling but unsupported by a direct citation or data reference; include specific source to substantiate the claim.
Figure 3 is informative but missing a descriptive caption and source attribution; standardize caption placement and format for consistency.
The denominator '19.715 mil milones' contains a typographical error ('mil milones'); correct to 'mil millones' for accurate mathematical presentation.
The 50% revenue allocation assumption for 2022 requires justification or sensitivity analysis to avoid unsupported estimation bias.
The inline calculation uses '6.344 mil milones' with unit mismatch; clarify million-unit notation consistently across equations.
